title: CAPM: If the expected return on a portfolio is 0.1 and...
summary: Calculate the expected return on equity using CAPM given beta, risk-free rate, and market return.
prompt: If the expected return on a portfolio is 10% and its beta is 0.8, what would the risk-free rate be if the expected market return is 12%?

options.text: Equal to the weighted average return
Using CAPM: E(r) = r_f + β[E(r_m) - r_f]

solution: Using CAPM: E(r) = r_f + β[E(r_m) - r_f]. Rearranging gives r_f = E(r) - β[E(r_m) - r_f].
